evidence:
From I-HY_2022_14-GCT-00: "# GCT 34 Tenderer’s consent and authorization on conviction records
The tenderer shall submit with the tender a duly signed letter in the form set out in Appendix E to these General Conditions of Tender giving consent to the Major Works Project Management Office of Highways Department to obtain from all relevant government departments/bureaux, authorizing such relevant government departments/bureaux to release and make available to the Major Works Project Management Office of Highways Department" From ECC HK_GCT34_20231121_clean.docx: "ay have any involvement therein.  For the purpose of this sub-paragraph, “serious bodily injury” and “construction site” shall bear the same meanings as assigned to them under paragraph 10(g)(ii) and paragraph 10(a), respectively, of DEVB TC(W) No. 5/2023 dated 28 July 2023 or any subsequent update.

	We give further consent to the [ name of the project office / procuring department ] to furnish the information described in sub-paragraphs (1) and (2) above to [ name of project consultant], the P"
reasoning: The STANDARD clause references consent related to 'serious bodily injury' and 'construction site' as defined in DEVB TC(W) No. 5/2023 and includes a provision for sharing information with the Project Manager designate, but does not specify conviction record disclosures under listed ordinances. The TENDER clause introduces a detailed requirement for submitting conviction records under 15 specific ordinances—content absent in the STANDARD clause. This constitutes a material addition/contradiction, not merely a modification.

These are explicit, documented adjustments.

evidence:

(iii) The activity schedule in either hard copy" From ECC HK_GCT4_FA_20231115.docx: "ocopying charge for tenders opened by the CTB and PWTB are $12.0 per page and $16.2 per page respectively.]
(4) | If a tenderer elects to submit the priced *bill of quantities/*activity schedule in hard copy format and where a hard copy has been supplied by the Client, it should price the *bill of quantities/*activity schedule on the hard copy supplied by the Client. If a tenderer fails to do so, any extra cost incurred by the Client in checking whether the printed descriptions or figures of the"
reasoning: The STANDARD clause states that non-evaluated hard copy parts need not be checked for compliance with any requirements. evidence:

Statement of convictions under the Immigration Ordinance (Cap. 115)
(1) A tender will not be considered if, during the 12-month period prior to the date set for the close of tender, or if this has been extended, the extended date, the tenderer has had three or more convictions in respect of separate incidents under Sections 17I or 38A of the Immigration Ordinance (Cap. 115) for employing illegal workers or for having illegal workers on any site under the tenderer’s control, whether or n" From ECC HK_GCT27_20231115.docx: "ts) during the 12-month^ period prior to the date set for the close of tender, or if this has been extended, the extended date, the details of which shall include the legislation violated, dates of offences, dates of convictions and the associated fine imposed by the court, site addresses, contract numbers and contract titles, or a statement of “no conviction”. reasoning: The TENDER clause narrows the scope of the STANDARD clause by specifying convictions only under Sections 17I or 38A of the Immigration Ordinance (Cap. 115) related to illegal workers, whereas the STANDARD clause appears broader (referring generally to 'legislation violated'). Additionally, the TENDER clause introduces a disqualification threshold (three or more convictions) not present in the STANDARD clause. These are explicit, documented adjustments.

evidence:
